steps.title: Locate the x-intercepts on the graph
steps.content: Distinct values of $x$ where $f(x) = 0$ correspond to points where the graph crosses or touches the x-axis. From the graph, the curve meets the x-axis at two marked points: $x = -3$ and $x = 1$.

steps.title: Count the distinct x-values
steps.content: There are exactly two distinct $x$-values, $x = -3$ and $x = 1$, where $f(x) = 0$. That confirms B is correct.
